goal: Ship P1-W3-F03 (ucil-daemon Watchman integration + backend selection + Poll fallback) on top of current main, baking in the crate-scoped `test_support::ENV_GUARD` / `PathRestoreGuard` pattern (per DEC-0011) so the PATH-mutation × git-spawn race that rejected WO-0027 three times cannot recur under the coverage-gate `cargo test` path.
plan_summary: The required watcher surface (`WatcherBackend`, `WatchmanCapability`, `detect_watchman`, `count_files_capped`, `auto_select_backend`, `WATCHMAN_AUTO_SELECT_THRESHOLD`, `POLL_WATCHER_INTERVAL`, `FileWatcher::new_with_backend`, plus the frozen nextest selector `watcher::test_watchman_detection`) is already fully specified by WO-0027 scope_in and was critic-CLEAN on the feat branch; the 14 implementation commits live on `feat/WO-0027-watchman-detection-and-backend-selection` with tip `036e9cf`. Because that branch is now 168 commits behind main and was never merged, WO-0039 re-lands the surface on a fresh branch from `main` (33cac2e). The NEW element relative to WO-0027 is the crate-scoped `test_support` module (new file `crates/ucil-daemon/src/test_support.rs`, registered under `#[cfg(test)] mod test_support;` in `lib.rs`) exposing `pub(crate) static ENV_GUARD: Mutex<()>`, `env_guard()`, and `PathRestoreGuard` per DEC-0011. Watcher PATH-mutating tests wrap their critical section in `PathRestoreGuard::new()`; session_manager tests that spawn `git` via `tokio::process::Command` (`detect_branch_*`, `discover_worktrees_*`, `create_session_*`, `get_session_returns_some_after_create`, the sibling `test_session_state_tracking`) acquire a bare `crate::test_support::env_guard()` for the duration of their git call. Executor is FREE to cherry-pick commits `9dcecbd..036e9cf` from `feat/WO-0027-...` onto the new branch as an accelerator — all scope_in paths are covered by that commit range — but the executor MUST verify the resulting branch compiles against current main and MUST have `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib` exit 0 before writing ready-for-review.
scope_in: Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add `which = "7"` under `[workspace.dependencies]` if not already present (cross-platform binary lookup; rust-analyzer / cargo precedent; v7 series current as of 2026-Q1). `walkdir = "2"` is ALREADY present at line 92.
crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — under `[dependencies]`, add `which = { workspace = true }` AND `walkdir = { workspace = true }` (daemon currently has neither; workspace provides walkdir).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/test_support.rs — NEW FILE. Crate-scoped test utilities compiled only under `#[cfg(test)]`. Mirrors `036e9cf` reference implementation: `pub(crate) static ENV_GUARD: Mutex<()> = Mutex::new(())`; `pub(crate) fn env_guard() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()>` (poison → inner via `unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner)`); `pub(crate) struct PathRestoreGuard { original: Option<OsString>, _lock: MutexGuard<'static, ()> }` with `new()` snapshotting `PATH` + holding the lock, and `Drop` restoring-or-clearing `PATH`. File-level rustdoc explains the coverage-gate vs nextest split and cites DEC-0011.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — add `#[cfg(test)] mod test_support;` near the other `pub mod` declarations (existing `pub mod watcher;` at ~line 46 is a good anchor; test_support is NOT re-exported outside the crate). Extend the existing `pub use watcher::{...}` line to include the new WO-0039 public items: `WatcherBackend`, `WatchmanCapability`, `detect_watchman`, `count_files_capped`, `auto_select_backend`, `WATCHMAN_AUTO_SELECT_THRESHOLD`, `POLL_WATCHER_INTERVAL`.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add the WO-0027 public surface on top of the existing 638-line WO-0026 foundation: `pub enum WatcherBackend { NotifyDebounced, Watchman, Poll }` (derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`); `pub struct WatchmanCapability { pub binary: PathBuf }` (derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq`); `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.daemon.watcher.detect_watchman", level = "debug")] pub fn detect_watchman() -> Option<WatchmanCapability>` (uses `which::which("watchman")`); `pub fn count_files_capped(root: &Path, cap: usize) -> usize` (`walkdir::WalkDir::new(root).into_iter().filter_map(Result::ok).filter(|e| e.file_type().is_file()).take(cap.saturating_add(1)).count()`); `pub fn auto_select_backend(root: &Path, threshold: usize) -> WatcherBackend` (Watchman iff detect_watchman && count > threshold, else NotifyDebounced); `pub const WATCHMAN_AUTO_SELECT_THRESHOLD: usize = 50_000;` (master-plan §2 line 138); `pub const POLL_WATCHER_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(2);`; `impl FileWatcher { pub fn new_with_backend(root: &Path, sender: mpsc::Sender<FileEvent>, backend: WatcherBackend) -> Result<Self, WatcherError> }` dispatching to three private constructors (notify-debounced, poll, watchman-subprocess); refactor existing `FileWatcher::new` body to a single-line delegate `Self::new_with_backend(root, sender, WatcherBackend::NotifyDebounced)` (WO-0026 call-sites must stay byte-identical). Extend `#[non_exhaustive] enum WatcherError` with `WatchmanSpawn(std::io::Error)` and `WatchmanDecode(serde_json::Error)` (both non-`#[from]`; `Io(#[from] std::io::Error)` arm is preserved as-is). Private `#[derive(Debug, serde::Deserialize)] pub(crate) struct WatchmanEvent { name: String, exists: bool, new: bool }` for JSON decoding. Watchman backend spawns `watchman --no-spawner --server-encoding=json --json-command` via `tokio::process::Command` (NOT `std::process::Command` per rust-style.md), writes the `["subscribe", <root>, "ucil-daemon", { ... }]` JSON, reads JSONL from stdout via `tokio::io::BufReader::lines()`, forwards each event as `FileEvent { path, kind: FileEventKind::Modified, source: EventSource::NotifyDebounced }`. Poll backend uses `notify::PollWatcher` wired through the existing debouncer-forwarder pipeline. `FileWatcher` holds a private `BackendHandle` enum owning the backend-specific resource (`Debouncer<RecommendedWatcher>`, `Debouncer<PollWatcher>`, or `tokio::process::Child { kill_on_drop: true }`).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs TESTS — all module-root `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` or `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` per DEC-0005 (no `mod tests { }` wrapper; matches the 8 existing WO-0026 watcher tests' shape):

* `test_watchman_detection` — **FROZEN F03 SELECTOR**. `#[cfg(all(test, unix))]`. `let _guard = crate::test_support::PathRestoreGuard::new();` at the TOP so the whole test holds the crate-wide mutex. Empty-PATH case: `std::env::set_var("PATH", tempdir1.path())` → assert `detect_watchman().is_none()`. Then create `tempdir1/watchman` with mode `0o755` via `std::fs::File::create(...) + set_permissions(Permissions::from_mode(0o755))`; re-assert `detect_watchman() == Some(WatchmanCapability { binary: <canonicalised> })`. The guard's Drop restores the inherited PATH.
* `test_count_files_capped_below_cap` — create 3 files via `std::fs::File::create` in a tempdir, assert `count_files_capped(root, 10) == 3`.
* `test_count_files_capped_stops_early` — create 20 files, assert `count_files_capped(root, 5) <= 6` (walker early-exits after cap+1).

* `test_auto_select_backend_returns_watchman_when_available_and_above_threshold` — `PathRestoreGuard::new()`; 11 files in tempdir-A; fake watchman shim in tempdir-B; PATH=tempdir-B; assert `auto_select_backend(tempdir_A, 10) == WatcherBackend::Watchman`.
* `test_auto_select_backend_returns_notify_when_below_threshold` — `PathRestoreGuard::new()`; 3 files + shim + PATH; assert result `== NotifyDebounced`.

* `test_poll_backend_delivers_events` — `#[tokio::test]`. No PATH mutation so no PathRestoreGuard. `FileWatcher::new_with_backend(&root, tx, WatcherBackend::Poll)?`; spawn a task that writes `root/poll-hello.txt` after 100 ms; loop on `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(10), rx.recv())` until an event whose `path` ends with `poll-hello.txt` arrives; assert `source == EventSource::NotifyDebounced` (Poll events flow through the same debouncer pipeline — document this in WatcherBackend::Poll rustdoc).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs TESTS-ONLY edits — add `let _g = crate::test_support::env_guard();` at the TOP of EACH of the following test functions, BEFORE any `SessionManager::*` call that reaches the `tokio::process::Command::new("git")` spawns at lines 291, 311, 339: `test_session_state_tracking` (module-root at line 432), and inside the `mod tests { }` block: `create_session_returns_fresh_uuid_each_call`, `detect_branch_returns_non_empty_inside_git_repo`, `detect_branch_errors_outside_git_repo` (this one still needs the guard: the `detect_branch` call spawns git, the error result comes from git saying 'not in a repo' — both require a sane PATH), `discover_worktrees_returns_at_least_one`, `get_session_returns_some_after_create`. Keep `_g` bound with a leading underscore so clippy's `unused_variables` lint passes without needing `#[allow]`. The `parse_worktree_porcelain_main_and_linked_and_detached` test does NOT spawn git (pure-string parse) and MUST NOT acquire the guard — adding an unnecessary lock would serialise a fast test. No other changes to this file; runtime methods `create_session`, `detect_branch`, `discover_worktrees` are untouched. Comment each `let _g = ...` line with `// DEC-0011: fence PATH mutations in watcher tests`.

Rustdoc on every new public item: `WatcherBackend`, `WatchmanCapability`, `detect_watchman`, `count_files_capped`, `auto_select_backend`, `WATCHMAN_AUTO_SELECT_THRESHOLD`, `POLL_WATCHER_INTERVAL`, `FileWatcher::new_with_backend`. Cite master-plan §2 line 138 (backend-selection policy) and §18 Phase 1 Week 3 (watcher feature scope). Use fully qualified links to avoid the `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` landmine (past WO-0009 / WO-0025 / WO-0027 pain). Do NOT use 2-letter method links where multiple same-named methods exist.

Commit ladder — approximately 8 commits in this order: (1) `build(workspace): add which workspace dep + walkdir to daemon deps`; (2) `feat(daemon): add crate-scoped test_support module for PATH mutex (DEC-0011)`; (3) `feat(daemon): add WatcherBackend enum + WatchmanCapability + constants`; (4) `feat(daemon): add detect_watchman + count_files_capped + auto_select_backend`; (5) `feat(daemon): extend WatcherError with WatchmanSpawn + WatchmanDecode variants`; (6) `feat(daemon): FileWatcher::new_with_backend dispatch to 3 backends (notify/poll/watchman)`; (7) `test(daemon): add 7 watcher tests under PathRestoreGuard`; (8) `test(daemon): fence session_manager git-spawning tests with env_guard (DEC-0011)`; (9, if lib.rs still needs a separate commit) `feat(daemon): re-export WO-0039 watcher public surface from lib.rs`. Soft target ≤50 lines/commit per .claude/rules/commit-style.md; the test-introduction commit may exceed under DEC-0001/DEC-0005 module-introduction precedent.
scope_out: NO edits to runtime (non-test) code in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs`. Only test functions gain the `let _g = ...;` guard line. Runtime methods `SessionManager::create_session`, `detect_branch`, `discover_worktrees` are BYTE-IDENTICAL.
NO auto-detection of network mounts — `PollWatcher` is an explicit opt-in via `WatcherBackend::Poll`. `/proc/mounts` / `statvfs` network-fs detection is deferred to a later WO.
NO `ucil-cli` / `ucil init` wiring — `auto_select_backend()` is exposed for a future CLI WO to call.
NO changes to existing `FileWatcher::new(root, sender)` signature — only body is a one-line delegate to `new_with_backend(..., NotifyDebounced)`.
NO changes to `FileEvent`, `FileEventKind`, `EventSource`, `notify_hook_event`, or `DEBOUNCE_WINDOW` — all frozen by WO-0026.
NO new crate, NO crate rename, NO new `[workspace.members]` entry.

NO watchman subscription integration test on this WO — unit-test-level detection only; a future integration-tester WO may exercise real subscription end-to-end on hosts with watchman installed (via docker fixture path). `#[ignore]` is FORBIDDEN per root CLAUDE.md.
NO `watchman_client` crate dep — watchman is spawned as a subprocess via `tokio::process::Command`.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, `it.skip`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.

NO edits to other daemon modules: `lifecycle.rs`, `plugin_manager.rs`, `server.rs`, `session_ttl.rs`, `storage.rs`, `main.rs`, `executor.rs`, `startup.rs`, `priority_queue.rs`, `text_search.rs`, `understand_code.rs`.
NO edits to other crates (`ucil-core`, `ucil-treesitter`, `ucil-lsp-diagnostics`, `ucil-embeddings`, `ucil-agents`, `ucil-cli`, adapters, ml, plugins, tests/integration).

NO `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper for the NEW watcher tests — module-root flat per DEC-0005 (mirrors the 8 existing WO-0026 watcher tests). The EXISTING `mod tests { }` in session_manager.rs is preserved (it predates DEC-0005 and is out of this WO's refactor scope); we only add `let _g = ...` lines inside its test bodies.
NO relaxation of clippy, rustfmt, or rustdoc gates. No per-test `#[allow]` for PATH manipulation — the `_g` underscore prefix is sufficient for `unused_variables`; no other lint should fire.
NO `std::env::set_var` calls in tests WITHOUT an active `PathRestoreGuard` or `env_guard()` hold — any such test would re-introduce the DEC-0011 race.

NO cherry-pick of verifier/critic/escalation commits from `feat/WO-0027-...` (`48c5d57`, `074e6df`, `8fc6335`, `592c908`, `de5039d`, `42aba9d`, `650c854` Cargo.lock regen). If the executor cherry-picks, restrict to the impl commits `9dcecbd`, `b81a723`, `37939f0`, `ce34234`, `d688cae`, `6a0e99b`, `7c5d903`, `b0f7a33`, `036e9cf` (9 commits; then Cargo.lock regen happens organically on first build).
acceptance: New Watchman-detection + backend-selection + test_support API exists: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs` exports the 8 public items listed in scope_in; `crates/ucil-daemon/src/test_support.rs` exists as a `#[cfg(test)]` module; `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` re-exports the watcher items and declares `#[cfg(test)] mod test_support;`.
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::test_watchman_detection'` → exactly 1 passing test (the frozen F03 selector).
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::'` → ≥13 passing tests (8 WO-0026 + 6 PATH-mutation + 1 Poll-backend = 15; ≥13 floor accounts for possible `#[cfg(windows)]` gating).

`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib` → exit 0 with no failures (THIS is the coverage-gate-equivalent path; rejected WO-0027 three times — DEC-0011 fix must land this green). No watcher or session_manager test may fail or panic.
`FileWatcher::new(root, sender)` signature unchanged and all existing WO-0026 `watcher::test_notify_*` / `test_hook_*` / `test_watcher_shutdown_is_clean` tests still pass.
`cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings` → clean, no new warnings.
`cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` → exit 0 with no `^error` and no `^warning: unresolved` lines.
Mutation check (verifier runs): stash the body of `detect_watchman()` (replace with `None`) → `test_watchman_detection` fails; pop → test passes.

DEC-0011 compliance: every `std::env::set_var("PATH"` or `std::env::remove_var("PATH"` in `crates/ucil-daemon/` (grep-verifiable) lies inside a test function that has acquired `PathRestoreGuard::new()` or `env_guard()` from `crate::test_support`.
acceptance_criteria: cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::test_watchman_detection' --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/nextest-WO-0039-F03.log && grep -Eq '1 test run: 1 passed|tests run: 1, 1 passed' /tmp/nextest-WO-0039-F03.log

cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::' --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/nextest-WO-0039-watcher.log && grep -Eq '(1[3-9]|[2-9][0-9]) tests run: (1[3-9]|[2-9][0-9]) passed|tests run: ([1-9][0-9]+|1[3-9]|[2-9][0-9])' /tmp/nextest-WO-0039-watcher.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib 2>&1 | tee /tmp/cargo-test-WO-0039.log && grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. [0-9]+ passed; 0 failed' /tmp/cargo-test-WO-0039.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy-WO-0039.log && ! grep -q '^error' /tmp/clippy-WO-0039.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps 2>&1 | tee /tmp/doc-WO-0039.log && ! grep -qE '^(error|warning: unresolved)' /tmp/doc-WO-0039.log

predecessor:ucil-build/work-orders/0027-watchman-detection-and-backend-selection.json (WO-0027's full implementation is on the unmerged feat branch at tip 036e9cf; this WO re-lands on main with DEC-0011 pre-applied — executor MAY cherry-pick the 9 impl commits 9dcecbd..036e9cf rather than re-type from scratch)
commit:036e9cf (reference test_support.rs implementation — copy verbatim onto the new branch; source of truth for the DEC-0011 pattern)

dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W3-F02: passes=true. WO-0026 landed on main at commit 7414c1df763c95889ab9bb12a8b0593237b119ea. crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs:1-638 provides FileWatcher, FileEvent (Created/Modified/Removed/Renamed/Other), EventSource (NotifyDebounced/PostToolUseHook), WatcherError (#[non_exhaustive]), DEBOUNCE_WINDOW, notify_hook_event — the foundation WO-0039 extends.

lessons_applied: WO-0027 × 3 rejections (592c908, de5039d, 42aba9d) — same PATH-mutation race. Fix at 036e9cf (crate-scoped test_support). DEC-0011 codifies the pattern. This WO bakes the fix in up-front: every test that mutates PATH holds PathRestoreGuard; every test that spawns git holds env_guard. The new acceptance criterion `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib` is the coverage-gate-equivalent path that rejected WO-0027.

DEC-0005 / WO-0006 / WO-0007 selector-mismatch avoidance: new watcher tests at module root under #[cfg(test)] #[test], matching the 8 existing WO-0026 watcher tests and the call_hierarchy / quality_pipeline precedents. The pre-existing `mod tests { }` block in session_manager.rs is preserved (out-of-scope refactor).

WO-0009 / WO-0025 / WO-0027 rustdoc intra-doc link landmine: acceptance_criteria[4] explicitly greps `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` for `^error` or `^warning: unresolved`; rustdoc uses fully qualified paths where ambiguity could arise.

WO-0012 / WO-0020 abandonment pattern avoidance: the acceptance-test green path does NOT depend on watchman being installed on the test host (the shim-via-tempdir pattern); executor/verifier loop cannot get stuck on environment absence.
WO-0024 rustdoc regression: explicit rustdoc gate in acceptance_criteria catches any new unresolved-link warning pre-verifier.

WO-0038 mixed-project fixture / planner-regex gap: acceptance_criteria[0..2] use grep -Eq with alternation that matches BOTH cargo-test and cargo-nextest summary line shapes (`test result: ok.`, `N tests run: M passed`) — no retry RCF lap needed for output-shape drift.
